title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 338.030 - Applicant — requirements for qualification
text:
338.030.  Applicant — requirements for qualification. — An applicant for examination shall be twenty-one years of age and in addition shall furnish satisfactory evidence of his good moral character and have had one year practical experience under the supervision of a licensed pharmacist within a licensed pharmacy, or other location approved by the board, and shall be a graduate of a school or college of pharmacy whose requirements for graduation are satisfactory to and approved by the board of pharmacy.
(RSMo 1939 § 10014, A. 1949 H.B. 2075, A.L. 1951 p. 737, A.L. 1981 S.B. 16, A.L. 1990 H.B. 1287, A.L. 2001 H.B. 567) 
Prior revisions: 1929 § 13142; 1919 § 4714; 1909 § 5766
